questions.scenario: A staging network works, but nobody can explain which defaults were accepted during creation. What is the main IaC concern?

options.text: The infrastructure choices are not reviewable or repeatable.
The cloud provider cannot run networks.
Every environment must be created from scratch every day.
The console is never useful for inspection.

questions.explanation: A working environment can still be hard to review, reproduce, or recover when the choices live only in memory.

questions.scenario: Production needs the same invoice bucket settings as staging. The only note says, `bucket exists, public access off`. What is missing?

options.text: The exact reviewed settings, ownership, permissions, and rebuild path.
A rule that forbids all storage services.
A requirement to memorize every provider API.
A screenshot of the prettiest dashboard.

questions.explanation: A short note is not the same as a reviewable source of truth for important infrastructure behavior.

questions.scenario: Which risks grow when routine infrastructure changes happen directly in the console?

options.text: Hidden defaults become hard to review.
Different engineers may recreate environments differently.
The team may lack a clear rebuild path.
Plain text files stop working everywhere.

questions.explanation: Console-first changes can hide defaults, make repeatability weak, and leave recovery dependent on memory.
